### QUESTION B (14 Marks) — [KTU University Exam – Supplementary 2023]

**(a)** Explain the concept of **lease** in the context of farm structures. Differentiate between **economic rent, contract rent, and gross rent**. **(7 marks)**

**(b)** A farm machinery shed is constructed for **₹12,50,000** under a hypothecation scheme. Salvage value = **₹1,25,000**, useful life = **20 years**, rate of interest = **10% p.a.**, annual repairs = **1% of capital cost**, insurance = **0.5% of capital cost**, supervision = **0.5% of capital cost**. Compute the **annual rent**. If rent is to be **revised every 5 years at 6% escalation**, calculate the **rent in the 6th year** and the **total 5-year rent collection**. **(7 marks)**

### Model Solution to Question B

#### Part (a) — Lease Concept and Rent Types

**Lease Definition:**
A **lease** is a contractual agreement in which the **lessor** (owner of the farm structure) grants the **lessee** (user) the right to occupy and use the structure for a defined period in exchange for **periodic payment (rent)**. The lessee does not acquire ownership; the asset returns to the lessor (or is renewed) at lease expiry.

**Differences between Rent Types:**

| Parameter | Economic Rent | Contract Rent | Gross Rent |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Definition** | Minimum theoretical rent to keep asset in use | Actual rent agreed in lease deed | Contract rent + tenant-borne charges |
| **Basis** | Mathematical (CRF + SFF + M) | Negotiation between parties | Contract rent + taxes + maintenance |
| **Used in** | Financial appraisal, audit | Legal lease deed | Tenant's actual outflow |
| **Variation** | Always positive | Can be below economic rent (subsidy) | Always ≥ contract rent |

#### Part (b) — Numerical Solution

**Given:**
- P = ₹12,50,000
- n = 20 years
- S = ₹1,25,000
- i = 0.10
- Repairs = 1% of P = ₹12,500
- Insurance = 0.5% of P = ₹6,250
- Supervision = 0.5% of P = ₹6,250
- Total M = ₹25,000
- Escalation e = 6% = 0.06

**Step 1:** Compute $(1.10)^{20}$

$$(1.10)^{20} = 6.7275$$

**Step 2:** Compute CRF

$$CRF = \dfrac{0.10 \times 6.7275}{6.7275 - 1} = \dfrac{0.67275}{5.7275} = 0.11746$$

**Step 3:** Compute SFF

$$SFF = \dfrac{0.10}{5.7275} = 0.01746$$

**Step 4:** Capital Recovery

$$A_{CR} = (12,50,000 - 1,25,000) \times 0.11746 = 11,25,000 \times 0.11746 = ₹1,32,143$$

**Step 5:** Sinking Fund

$$A_{SF} = 1,25,000 \times 0.01746 = ₹2,183$$

**Step 6:** Maintenance

$$M = 25,000$$

**Step 7:** Annual Rent (Base Year)

$$R_0 = 1,32,143 + 2,183 + 25,000 = ₹1,59,326$$

**Step 8:** Rent in 6th Year (5 years of escalation applied)

$$R_5 = R_0 \times (1.06)^5 = 1,59,326 \times 1.3382 = ₹2,13,212$$

**Step 9:** Total 5-Year Rent Collection

$$\text{Total} = R_0 \times \dfrac{(1.06)^5 - 1}{0.06} = 1,59,326 \times \dfrac{1.3382 - 1}{0.06}$$

$$\text{Total} = 1,59,326 \times 5.6371 = ₹8,98,231$$

> [!NOTE]
> **Final Answer:**
> - Annual Rent (Base) = **₹1,59,326**
> - Rent in 6th Year = **₹2,13,212**
> - Total 5-Year Collection = **₹8,98,231**

## 5.3 KTU Examiner's Valuation Warning / Pitfall Callout

> [!WARNING]
> **Common Mark-Loss Pitfalls — Read Carefully!**
> 1. **Forgetting the Sinking Fund Component:** Many students write only R = P·CRF + M. This is the most common mistake, costing **3 out of 7 marks** in numericals.
> 2. **Confusing CRF and SFF Numerators:** CRF numerator has $(1+i)^n$ factor; SFF does not. Memorizing wrongly loses 2 marks.
> 3. **Using P instead of (P − S) in CRF term:** Capital recovery should be on **depreciated value**, not full value. Common error.
> 4. **Not converting percentage to decimal:** Writing i = 10 instead of i = 0.10 in the formula — answer goes wildly off.
> 5. **Skipping units in final answer:** Always write **₹ per annum** explicitly. Examiners deduct for missing units.
> 6. **Ignoring salvage value in exam question:** If the problem states salvage = ₹X, you **MUST** use it. Ignoring it = full marks lost.
> 7. **Year-of-escalation indexing error:** Rent "in 6th year" means **k = 5** escalations have been applied (Year 1 is base, Year 2 = 1 escalation, etc.). Common confusion.
> 8. **Mixing up lease types:** Confusing lease with licence, or rent with revenue — these terminological errors are penalized in Part A.

## 5.4 Topic Recap & Important Things to Remember

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **HIGH-DENSITY RAPID REVISION CHECKLIST**

- [x] **Hypothecation** = pledge of asset as collateral **without possession transfer**
- [x] **Three rent components** = Capital Recovery + Sinking Fund + Maintenance
- [x] **CRF formula** = $\dfrac{i(1+i)^n}{(1+i)^n - 1}$ (used with depreciated value P − S)
- [x] **SFF formula** = $\dfrac{i}{(1+i)^n - 1}$ (used with salvage value S)
- [x] **Master Rent Equation** = $R = (P-S)\cdot CRF + S\cdot SFF + M$
- [x] **Standard economic life** = 30 yrs (godown), 25 yrs (cattle shed), 15 yrs (greenhouse)
- [x] **Standard interest rate** = 9%–12% p.a. in Indian agricultural schemes
- [x] **Standard maintenance** = 1%–2% of capital cost per year
- [x] **Rent revision** every 3–5 years with 5%–6% escalation
- [x] **Three rent types** = Economic, Contract, Gross — know the difference
- [x] **Lease tenure** = Short-term (1–5 yr), Long-term (10–30 yr), Perpetual (99+ yr)
- [x] **Stakeholders** = Lessor, Lessee, Financier, Inspection Authority
- [x] **Sub-leasing** generally **prohibited** in farm hypothecation agreements
- [x] **NABARD and State Agriculture Departments** are the primary users of this method
- [x] **Always convert** interest rate percentage to decimal before substituting in formula
- [x] **Always state units** (₹/year) in final answer
- [x] **Salvage value** must NEVER be ignored if given in the problem
- [x] **Audit and accountability** — government lessors must show rent ≥ calculated economic rent
